title: Value Innovation

content.statement: Pursue differentiation and low cost simultaneously to create a leap in buyer value.
content.question: Which factors should we eliminate, reduce, raise, and create to make competition irrelevant?
content.equation: Value Innovation = (New Buyer Value Created) × (Cost Reduced)
content.mental_model: Two rails of a track, value goes up while cost comes down at the same time.
content.business_lens: Use the Four Actions on every offer: which industry factors do we eliminate, reduce, raise, and create?
content.life_lens: Stop trying to be marginally better at the same game; redesign the game so the trade-offs disappear.
sop: List every factor your industry competes on
Ask the four questions: Eliminate, Reduce, Raise, Create
Reconfigure the offer to drop costly factors customers do not value
Add factors that create new value
Iterate using a strategy canvas to visualize differentiation vs price
content.explainer: Cirque du Soleil eliminated star performers and animal acts (lower cost) while creating theatrical narrative and artistic music (higher value). The result was a new market category where traditional circuses could not compete on the same axes. The Four Actions framework forces you to look beyond incremental improvement.
questions: Which factors should we eliminate, reduce, raise, and create to make competition irrelevant?
What does everyone in your industry assume is required that customers don't actually value?
What could you add that no competitor offers, even if it means dropping something they all compete on?

definitions.def: A strategy that pursues differentiation and low cost at the same time, creating a leap in value for buyers instead of trading one for the other.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. New Buyer Value Created 8, Cost Reduced 7. Score = 8 times 7 = 56 out of a possible 100. Higher means you're creating a genuine leap in value while structurally lowering cost, a new market. Lower means you're still trading value for cost like everyone else.
content.closing_statement: Redesign the game instead of playing it better.

caption:
Principle Nº 271: Value Innovation

Pursue differentiation and low cost simultaneously to create a leap in buyer value. Cirque du Soleil eliminated star performers and animal acts (lower cost) while creating theatrical narrative and artistic music (higher value).

Ask yourself: Which factors should we eliminate, reduce, raise, and create to make competition irrelevant?
